title: rhododendron brachycarpum subsp. fauriei
intro: Rhododendron brachycarpum subsp. fauriei, also known as Korean azalea, is a subspecies of rhododendron native to Korea and China. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The health benefits associated with Rhododendron brachycarpum subsp. fauriei include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
